resources.string: Point your phone at the camera\'s Connection wizard showing its SSID and key.
Camera access is needed to read the Connection wizard.
Allow Camera in Android Settings, then return here to scan.
OpenZCine uses the rear camera only while this scanner is open.
Looking for a complete SSID and key. No image or recognized text leaves this phone.
Review these camera values before using them.
Check these against the camera screen. Nothing is saved until you choose Join camera Wi‑Fi.
Scanned network key. Check the camera screen before using it.
The rear camera could not start. Close other camera apps and try again.
On-device text recognition is not ready. Try again in a moment.
The shared camera core is unavailable. Rebuild OpenZCine before scanning.
Camera viewfinder scanning the Connection wizard
Only what pairing needs — change anytime in Android Settings.
Each trades battery, quality, and convenience — pick what fits the shoot.
Menu names match the Nikon ZR; they may vary by model and firmware version.
Get both devices onto the same network — we\'ll find the camera automatically.
Keep the camera powered on and nearby while we find it.
On the camera: Network menu → Connect to computer → Network settings.
Choose Create Profile and give the profile a name.
Select Direct connection to computer — the camera shows its SSID and key.
Turn on your phone\'s Wi‑Fi hotspot (Settings → Hotspot & tethering).
Note the hotspot name and password — the camera needs them to join.
On the camera: Network menu → Connect to computer.
We\'ll walk through joining your hotspot in the next step.
On the camera: turn on Connect to computer and keep the camera powered on.
Use a data-capable USB-C cable — charging-only cables cannot carry PTP.
Connect the camera directly to this phone, then approve Android\'s USB prompt.
The key for this camera is remembered — check the SSID and tap Join.
Enter the SSID and key from the camera\'s screen — we remember the key for next time.
Keep your phone\'s Wi‑Fi hotspot on. Then on the camera:
USB-C pairing does not use a Wi‑Fi network.
Search for Wi‑Fi network → join this phone\'s hotspot
Find and stream from your camera over Wi‑Fi.
Android uses Location access to find cameras on Wi‑Fi. Your location is never tracked or stored.
Couldn\'t reach the camera. Check Wi‑Fi and try again.
USB-C camera support is unavailable on this device.
This camera did not expose a stable USB identity. Reconnect it or use Wi‑Fi pairing.
Couldn\'t start the USB-C camera session. Reconnect the cable and try again.
Couldn\'t reach the USB-C camera. Check the cable and camera connection, then try again.
Couldn\'t join the camera\'s Wi‑Fi. Check the key and try again.
Choose the connection path that fits the shoot.
We\'ll walk you through it — your camera is connected in about a minute.
You can change this path later in Settings.
Android asks you to confirm the connection the first time.
Keep the camera powered on — this takes a few seconds.
Required to connect to your camera — grant it to continue.
Uses this phone\'s rear camera and on-device text recognition only.
SCANNED FROM CAMERA — CHECK BEFORE JOINING
Scanned network key. Check the camera screen before joining.
Waiting for the camera to join this phone\'s hotspot
The camera appears here a few seconds after it joins.
Connect a powered camera with a data-capable USB-C cable.
View assist rail. Swipe up for more tools.
Status shows timecode, media, and camera health. Tap a camera tile or value to change it.
Status shows recording, timecode, format, media, and feed health. Tap ISO, shutter, iris, focus, or WB below to change it.
Tap the slider to open View Assist. Tap a tool to turn it on, or press and hold a configurable tool for quick settings.
Tap a tool to turn it on. Press and hold a configurable tool for quick settings. Swipe for more.
Lock prevents accidental changes. DISP changes the monitor layout. Red records. Media opens clips. Gear opens Operator Setup.
Will show on the first real frame
HDR LIVE VIEW · IMAGE ASSISTS OFF
LIVE COLOR FORMAT · IMAGE ASSISTS OFF
. Another camera change is in progress.
Controls are unavailable until the camera is connected and unlocked.
ISO is unavailable during recording until codec readback completes.
ISO is locked while recording in R3D NE.
Waiting for the camera codec before offering ISO values.
Waiting for the active base-ISO circuit.
This camera did not provide ISO values for the active circuit.
Shutter is locked on the camera.
Waiting for the camera shutter lock state.
Waiting for the active shutter mode.
This control is locked on the camera.
This camera did not provide apertures for the mounted lens.
This camera did not provide white-balance choices.
This camera did not advertise recording modes.
This camera did not advertise codec modes.
This camera did not advertise movie VR modes.
This camera did not provide movie focus modes.
This camera did not provide movie focus areas.
This camera did not provide focus-subject modes.
This camera did not provide audio-sensitivity choices.
This microphone sensitivity has no Android write selector.
This camera did not provide audio-input choices.
This camera did not advertise a wind-filter control.
This camera did not advertise an input attenuator.
This camera did not advertise 32-bit-float audio.
This camera did not advertise dual-base ISO circuits.
This camera did not advertise shutter display modes.
This camera did not advertise its shutter lock control.
Fine tune is unavailable for the active white-balance mode.
Camera Grid Display is read-only on Android.
Tone follows the active recording codec.
Electronic VR is unavailable for this camera or active codec.
Connect to a camera to read this control.
This camera has not reported this control.
Command dashboard. Swipe up to view Image, Focus, and Audio controls.
Image, Focus, and Audio controls. Swipe up to reveal more command controls.
. %1$s Long press and drag to reorder.
. Controls are locked. Long press and drag to reorder.
. Double tap to change; long press and drag to reorder.
More command controls below. Swipe up to reveal them.
Open Settings, restore hidden monitor controls
%1$s did not confirm %2$s. Check the active camera mode and try again.
The camera rejected the control change.
The camera rejected the focus-point change.
The camera could not reset focus.
Live view active. Focus point position locked in app.
Live view active. Tap to move focus point; hold to lock its position.
Live view active. Camera focus point control unavailable.
Reset focus point unavailable while moving focus
2 scopes max in fit view. Close one or pinch to fill.
2 scopes max in fit view. Close one or rotate to landscape.
No active camera. Link measurements appear only after a session connects.
These choices restart only the disposable live-view JPEG stream through Swift. Recording format and card writes stay unchanged.
Reconnect returns to the saved-camera owner so Wi-Fi and USB cleanup stay scoped to the active profile.
Choose the built-in monitor look used when LUT is enabled.
Choose the camera-aware scale and optional movable reference display.
Shows the Swift-derived palette key only while False Color is active.
LUT, False Color, Peaking, and Zebra require Android 13 or newer and the bundled Swift core.
Unavailable on this device. Scope and framing assists remain fully functional.
Configure the shared Swift meter used by the histogram and goal-post panel.
Show small RGB edge blocks for crushed and clipped channels.
Forwarded to the shared Swift meter; Kotlin only stores and presents this choice.
OpenZCine draws these monitor-only overlays over the visible feed. They never change the camera\'s Grid Display setting.
Guides, masks, grids, crosshair, and desqueeze are local display assists. Camera Grid Display remains camera-owned and unchanged.
Uses the camera virtual horizon when its live-view header reports a reliable value.
If the camera does not provide a level, the monitor may use labelled device tilt fallback.
Sensitivity and color are resolved by the shared Swift exposure-assist facade.
Highlight and midtone warnings use the active camera curve; 0–255 changes only the editor.
Mode, trace brightness, guides, and footprint are applied by the clean-frame Canvas view.
RGB or YRGB channel layout with independent trace and guide controls.
Zoom changes core density binning; brightness and footprint affect only the rendered trace.
Histogram footprint and shared edge warnings remain independently persisted.
This LUT could not be prepared.
Choose one .cube document. Swift validates it before a bounded app-private copy; OpenZCine never keeps the document URI or scans storage.
No custom LUTs yet. Import a .cube to add a monitor look.
Only LUTs added by an authorized RED workflow appear here; none are bundled.
Only OpenZCine\'s app-private copy will be removed. The document you selected from storage is untouched.
OpenZCine could not remove that private LUT copy.
RED assets are never bundled. Download remains unavailable until RED authorizes an Android endpoint and terms workflow.
Local framing assists. %1$s %2$s %3$s %4$s %5$s %6$s Camera Grid Display is unchanged.
Monitor-only; Android cannot reliably distinguish Bluetooth from phone hardware keys.
Volume Up/Down and Play/Pause toggle recording; Record/Play start and Pause/Stop stop. Confirmation is skipped.
Show only the monitor regions you need. Settings stays reachable when landscape rails are hidden.
Show or hide tools, then drag a trailing handle to set their monitor order.
Hide readouts you do not ride during a take.
Drag to reorder; disable modes you do not use. One mode always remains.
Couldn\'t read the local media cache.
Completed offline cache only. Camera originals, share files, accounts, and transfers stay untouched.
Offline camera cache only. Nothing on the camera is deleted.
Incomplete transfers stay in place so they can resume safely.
Clearing removes completed offline camera files only. Share files, accounts, and camera originals stay untouched.
Completed offline camera files will be removed from this phone. Camera originals, share files, accounts, and incomplete transfers stay untouched.
Couldn\'t clear the local media cache.
No completed cache files to remove.
This Android build is intentionally unavailable until an approved Adobe Native App client and exact redirect are supplied.
Adobe PKCE sign-in. Delivery accepts only final cache copies; native Share is unchanged.
Adobe PKCE sign-in and project selection for complete cached media. On a camera access point, Media requires an explicit hop and verifies the saved-camera reconnect afterward.
No app is available for that action.
Open project support, submit an anonymous Android problem report, or share a local report you can review first.
Send an anonymous Android report without a GitHub account.
Summary and what happened are required. Steps are optional but help reproduce it.
Only coarse app and Android details plus these choices are sent.
No diagnostics, logs, screenshots, media, or contact details are attached.
Sending creates a public GitHub issue. No account is needed and the report is anonymous, so OpenZCine cannot reply privately. Never include passwords, pairing codes, Wi-Fi details, or private media.
Do not report security vulnerabilities here. Follow SECURITY.md and use a private GitHub Security Advisory instead.
Keep this to %1$d characters or fewer.
Couldn\'t send this report. Your text is still here; try again later.
Anonymous reports are unavailable in this build. Your text is still here.
Too many reports were sent. Try again later.
Too many reports were sent. Try again in about %1$d minutes.
This report is too large to send. Shorten what happened or the reproduction steps.
Your anonymous report was sent for public triage. It has no contact details, so OpenZCine cannot reply privately.
Replay the short camera-controls, View Assist, and system-controls introduction without enabling camera commands underneath it.
Show Live View Guide on Next Real Frame
OpenZCine is open source. These links leave the app only when you choose them.
Build and protocol details for support reports.
